title: Batch per-file git metrics into single-pass scans
context: Indexing spawned two git log processes per file (2 × 395 = 790 spawns). Process creation dominated wall-time (23.8s observed, only 0.15s actual CPU). Windows process overhead made this pattern untenable for large repos.
decision: Replace O(files) individual git spawns with one fileGitMetrics function: single windowed git log to batch-count churn, single newest-first log with early-exit once all requested paths resolved. Parse once, populate map for all files.
consequences: Larger buffer required (256MB) for all-history name-only stream
Parsing complexity increased: NUL-prefixed commit markers, line-by-line matching against wanted set
All requested files get metrics simultaneously (no per-file lookups)
alternatives_rejected: Incremental per-file optimization (still O(files) fundamental limit)
Higher-level result caching (problem is query count, not redundant queries)
Parallel git spawns (still N processes, amplified resource contention on Windows)
